title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. nobilis
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. nobilis, also known as the wild daffodil or Lent lily, is a subspecies of the common daffodil plant that is native to the United Kingdom. This plant contains compounds with medicinal properties, including alkaloids and glycosides, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. nobilis include its potential to act as a diuretic, to reduce inflammation, and to have antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as arthritis and urinary tract infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tinctures, infusions, and salves, and should be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
